notes: Test prompts for the architecture-audit skill. Each entry has a prompt, the expected qualitative result, and optional input files. Assertions are not yet defined — they're drafted during the skill-creator eval loop.

evals.label: this-repo growing
evals.prompt: Review the architecture of this codebase. I want a written audit that calls out strengths and weaknesses, with prioritized recommendations.
evals.expected_output: Detects Umbraco 17, classifies lifecycle as growing (or asks once if ambiguous). High Pillar 5 (documentation & onboarding) score because of CLAUDE.md, ROADMAP.md, _specs/_plans/_features/ scaffolding. Low Pillar 2 (architectural separation) score — flat MVC layout, no layering. Mid-to-low Pillar 4 (headless suitability) score with 'firmly traditional / would require significant rewrite' note. Strong Pillar 6 signals around schema discipline (.uda artifacts). Recommendations include at least one P0 around a missing layer/seam and one P1 around documentation freshness. Report saved to _audits/<date>-this-repo.md.

evals.label: vanilla starter (greenfield)
evals.prompt: I just scaffolded a new Umbraco site from the Clean Starter template. Can you audit it?
evals.expected_output: Detects Umbraco (version-specific), classifies lifecycle as greenfield (low commit count, starter fingerprints intact). Foundational P0 recommendations: layering plan, ADR scaffolding, CI/pre-commit, secret-placeholder discipline. Low scores on most pillars but explicitly framed as 'greenfield is the ideal time to set these up.' Should NOT recommend a headless migration unprompted.

evals.label: comparison mode
evals.prompt: Audit our architecture and compare it to my colleague's Umbraco site at <external-path>. I want to see how we stack up.
evals.expected_output: Two audits produced and saved separately. A 'Comparison' section appears at the bottom of the primary report with side-by-side scores, at least one 'this repo wins' finding, and at least one 'other repo wins' finding. The 'What to learn from each' section is concrete, not generic.

evals.label: brownfield
evals.prompt: I just inherited an Umbraco codebase from a previous developer. Can you help me figure out what I'm working with?
evals.expected_output: Classifies as brownfield (user-declared via the 'inherited' phrasing). Report leads with a 'Brownfield map' section listing where things live, what was inferred vs known, and where the surprises are. Recommendations emphasize 'understand before changing' and risk-prioritize. No wholesale refactor P0s. Skill suggests the new owner write down what they learn (a BROWNFIELD-NOTES.md or equivalent).

evals.label: headless-leaning fixture
evals.prompt: How well is this site positioned to go headless?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers (description tuning test). Pillar 4 dominates the report. Detects Delivery API enablement, frontend project siblings, and content-access mediation. Produces a clear current-orientation + migration-readiness verdict.

evals.label: trigger accuracy — vague prompt
evals.prompt: Is our codebase set up the right way?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Asks one quick clarifying question if the working directory contents are ambiguous (no Umbraco detected). Otherwise proceeds with the default audit.
